en_body:
A flower-branch indifferent upon the piano keys
The blossom spreads wings, its sharp thorns still green
You have gone home, yet imprisoned the wind behind
Light wind...orchid fragrance...
Passing wind...orchid fragrance...
You leave forgetting to take heaven and earth
months-and-years echo the celestial chords
heaven and earth drunkened with melody
Leaving me seated at one corner of the cloud's foot
My heart paints a rainbow
Cloud-plumes rose, violet
Utmost velvet-longing lifted onto the strings,
Music-sound...the color green...
Music-chord...the color blue...

Please Accept My Thanks
A flower-branch indifferent upon the piano keys
The blossom spreads wings, its sharp thorns still green
You have gone home, yet imprisoned the wind behind
Light wind...orchid fragrance...
Passing wind...orchid fragrance...
You leave forgetting to take heaven and earth
months-and-years echo the celestial chords
heaven and earth drunkened with melody
Leaving me seated at one corner of the cloud's foot
My heart paints a rainbow
Cloud-plumes rose, violet
Utmost velvet-longing lifted onto the strings,
Music-sound...the color green...
Music-chord...the color blue...
note: The Vietnamese color “lơ” names a pale sky-blue, here distinguished from the preceding green.
